$comment: Model manifest. Entries describe every on-device ML model the app can use. bundled=true models ship in assets/models/bundled/. bundled=false models are downloaded to ApplicationDocumentsDirectory/models/ on first use and sha256-verified.

models.purpose: MediaPipe Selfie Segmentation — ML Kit bundles this internally; not loaded via ModelRegistry
Face bounding box detection — ML Kit bundles this internally; not loaded via ModelRegistry
MediaPipe Face Mesh — 468 3D landmarks from a pre-cropped face (input 192x192 sRGB in [0,1]). Source: storage.googleapis.com/mediapipe-assets/face_landmark.tflite

models.$comment: Phase XIII.6: DeepLab V3 MobileNetV2 trained on ADE20K (151 classes = unlabeled + 150 ADE20K scene-parsing categories, SKY=3). Input [1,513,513,3] float32, output [1,513,513,151]. Source: kaggle.com/api/v1/models/spsayakpaul/deeplabv3-mobilenetv2/tfLite/ade20k/1/download (unpacked from tar.gz). Paired with deeplab_v3_pascal: pascal rejects non-sky objects, ADE20K provides positive sky detection — union gives the sky replacement pipeline full coverage on cloudy / sunset / night-sky cases the heuristic misses.
models.purpose: DeepLab V3 MobileNetV2 ADE20K 151-class segmentation — sky class = 3. Positive-signal sky detection for sky replacement.

models.$comment: Phase XII.C.1: planned `deeplabv3_sky` was never published as a pre-trained tflite we could verify. Ship the MediaPipe PASCAL-VOC DeepLab V3 (21-class) instead — it doesn't have a direct sky class, but detecting person/car/animal/furniture pixels lets us *exclude* them from the sky mask, which is the high-leverage failure case on portraits-with-sky. Sky replacement still relies on the heuristic + top-bias signal for positive sky detection; the model acts as a non-sky-object filter.
models.purpose: DeepLab V3 PASCAL VOC 21-class semantic segmentation (bg + 20 object classes). Used as a non-sky-object filter inside sky replace. Source: storage.googleapis.com/mediapipe-models/image_segmenter/deeplab_v3/float32/latest/deeplab_v3.tflite

models.$comment: Phase XIV.1: EfficientDet-Lite0 with TFLite_Detection_PostProcess baked in (NMS + top-25 selection on-graph). Input [1,320,320,3] uint8 quantized to normalized [-1,1], outputs 4 tensors: locations [1,25,4] (ymin/xmin/ymax/xmax normalized), classes [1,25] float32 index, scores [1,25] float32, num_detections [1] float32. 90-class COCO (class ids from the COCO 2017 labelmap). Preferred over the MediaPipe `object_detector/int8/latest` build because that one exposes raw SSD heads — this variant already handles anchor decode + NMS so the Dart path just reads arrays. Source: kaggle.com/api/v1/models/tensorflow/efficientdet/tfLite/lite0-detection-default/1/download (unpacked from tar.gz).
models.purpose: EfficientDet-Lite0 COCO object detection (NMS baked in). Drives smart-crop suggestion + scanner prior region.
General background/object matting (lightweight U2-Net)
Ultra-lightweight 3x super-resolution fallback

models.$comment: Phase XIII.7: unblocked by Kaggle's API v1 direct-download endpoint which serves the model inside a tar.gz — fetched once manually, unpacked, and bundled directly. Keeps the service-load path identical to every other bundled tflite: ModelRegistry resolves to the asset path, LiteRtRuntime copies to a temp file and builds the interpreter. Source: kaggle.com/api/v1/models/google/arbitrary-image-stylization-v1/tfLite/256-int8-transfer/1/download.
models.purpose: Magenta arbitrary-style transfer (transfer half of the two-model pipeline)
LaMa object removal / inpainting
General portrait matting at 63 FPS on mobile GPU
4x super-resolution via Real-ESRGAN
Magenta style-prediction half — extracts style vectors from reference images

models.$comment: Phase XV.2: MediaPipe Selfie Multiclass segmenter (float32 TFLite). Input [1,256,256,3] float32 in [0,1], output [1,256,256,6] float32. Six classes: 0=background, 1=hair, 2=body-skin, 3=face-skin, 4=clothes, 5=others/accessories. Drives the hair/clothes recolour editor action via LAB a*b* shift on masked pixels. Downloadable because the model is 16 MB and shipping it bundled would swell the app install size noticeably — network fetch is a one-time cost. Source: storage.googleapis.com/mediapipe-models/image_segmenter/selfie_multiclass_256x256/float32/latest/selfie_multiclass_256x256.tflite
models.purpose: MediaPipe selfie multiclass segmenter (bg/hair/skin/clothes). Drives hair/clothes recolour.

models.$comment: Phase XV.1: Robust Video Matting (MobileNetV3 backbone, fp32 ONNX). Inputs src [1,3,H,W] + four recurrent-state tensors r1i..r4i + downsample_ratio [1]. Outputs fgr [1,3,H,W] + pha [1,1,H,W] + four r1o..r4o state tensors. For single-image matting we pass 1×1×1×1 zero tensors for r1i..r4i so the network runs in 'first frame' mode; the recurrent outputs are discarded. downsample_ratio = 0.25 matches 1080p-class inputs (RVM docs). Source: github.com/PeterL1n/RobustVideoMatting/releases/download/v1.0.0/rvm_mobilenetv3_fp32.onnx — chosen over fp16 so no fp16→fp32 conversion is needed in Dart (onnxruntime_v2 binds float32 natively).
models.purpose: Robust Video Matting (MobileNetV3 fp32) — best-in-class hair / fur / motion-blur edges for general matting.
High-quality general matting (RMBG-1.4 quantized)

models.$comment: Phase XVI.40: Depth-Anything-V2-Small INT8 quantised ONNX. Drives the depth-aware Lens Blur (the marquee differentiator vs. Snapseed). Inputs: src [1,3,518,518] float32 ImageNet-normalized RGB (mean=[0.485,0.456,0.406], std=[0.229,0.224,0.225]). Outputs: depth [1,518,518] float32 inverse-depth (higher=closer). Bundled because (a) lens blur is a high-touch user feature where first-use download latency would feel broken, (b) at ~12 MB INT8 it's similar to existing bundled assets (efficientdet_lite0=4.5MB, deeplab_v3_pascal=2.8MB, magenta_int8=284KB) and the bundle hits remain ~25 MB combined. The sha256 is a placeholder; verify and fill via `shasum -a 256 assets/models/bundled/depth_anything_v2_small_int8.onnx` before release. Source: huggingface.co/onnx-community/depth-anything-v2-small/resolve/main/onnx/model_quantized.onnx
models.purpose: Depth-Anything-V2-Small monocular depth estimation (518×518 INT8). Drives depth-aware Lens Blur.

models.$comment: Phase XVI.50: DnCNN-color INT8 denoiser ONNX (substitute for FFDNet per user direction in XVI.50). Inputs: 'input' [1,3,1024,1024] float32 in [0,1]. Outputs: [1,3,1024,1024] float32 — clean image when the export uses direct prediction, noise residual when residual-learning. AiDenoiseService.residualOutput selects the postprocessing branch. Downloaded because the file is ~7 MB and the existing bilateral-denoise slider already gives users a non-AI option (XVI.36 guided filter), so first-use download is acceptable. The URL field stays null until the user picks a specific community ONNX export to bundle against — sha256 is then verified via `shasum -a 256` and pinned. Until then this entry is in the deferredDownloadables allow-list of test/ai/manifest_integrity_test.dart.
models.purpose: DnCNN-color denoise (substitute for FFDNet). Adds an AI-tier 'Denoise (AI)' button that pre-processes the source proxy before the shader chain.

models.$comment: Phase XVI.51: MI-GAN inpainting (Sargsyan et al. 2023, ECCV — Picsart). Mobile-grade alternative to LaMa with comparable PSNR at ~7× smaller size and ~4× lower latency on phone CPUs. Drives the 'Fast' tier in the inpaint picker; LaMa stays as the 'Quality' tier. Inputs: image [1,3,512,512] float32 in [0,1] + mask [1,1,512,512] float32 in {0,1}. Outputs: [1,3,512,512] float32. Sanster's IOPaint mirror is the recommended community export — well-maintained, used by many open-source inpainting tools daily. Source URL: https://huggingface.co/Sanster/MIGAN/resolve/main/migan_pipeline_v2.onnx — the URL field stays null and sha256 placeholder until the user verifies the actual download via `shasum -a 256` and pins both. Until then this entry is in the deferredDownloadables allow-list.
models.purpose: MI-GAN mobile inpainting (Sanster IOPaint mirror). Faster + smaller alternative to LaMa for the inpaint flow.

models.$comment: Phase XVI.52: SegFormer-B0 (Xie et al. 2021) ADE20K-trained sky segmenter. Higher-quality alternative to the bundled DeepLabV3-MobileNetV2 ADE20K model (deeplab_v3_ade20k) — ~3 mIoU points better on the official ADE20K validation set, especially on soft-edged sky boundaries. Drives the 'high quality' sky-detection toggle alongside the existing DeepLab path. Inputs: 'pixel_values' [1,3,512,512] float32 ImageNet-normalized (mean=[0.485,0.456,0.406], std=[0.229,0.224,0.225]). Outputs: 'logits' [1,150,128,128] float32 — sky class index 2 per the SceneParse150 label list (note: the existing DeepLab variant uses class 3 because that model includes an 'unlabeled' index 0). Source URL: https://huggingface.co/onnx-community/segformer-b0-finetuned-ade-512-512/resolve/main/onnx/model_quantized.onnx — verify and pin sha256 before release. Until then this entry is in the deferredDownloadables allow-list.
models.purpose: SegFormer-B0 ADE20K sky segmenter (high quality). Alternative to the bundled DeepLabV3 ADE20K model for the sky-replace flow.

models.$comment: Phase XVI.53: Real-ESRGAN-x2plus FP16 ONNX. Doubles input dimensions; ~17 MB FP16 vs the bundled real_esrgan_x4 (~67 MB FP32). Drives the 'Enhance 2× (Fast)' default tier in the super-res picker; x4 stays as the 'Enhance 4× (Slow)' power-user toggle. Inputs: 'input' [1,3,256,256] float32 in [0,1] CHW (Real-ESRGAN convention; NO ImageNet normalisation). Outputs: [1,3,512,512] float32 in [0,1] CHW. Source URL: https://huggingface.co/onnx-community/Real-ESRGAN-x2plus/resolve/main/onnx/model_fp16.onnx (community export of xinntao/Real-ESRGAN x2plus weights). Verify and pin sha256 before release. Until then this entry is in the deferredDownloadables allow-list.
models.purpose: Real-ESRGAN-x2plus mobile super-resolution. Faster + smaller default tier alongside the existing x4 service.

models.$comment: Phase XVI.54: Harmonizer (Ke et al. 2022, ECCV — github.com/ZHKKKe/Harmonizer). White-box filter regressor (~2M params, ~8 MB ONNX) that predicts 8 photo-editing parameters — brightness/contrast/saturation/temperature/tint/sharpness/highlights/shadows — to harmonise a composited subject against its new background. Plugs into compose_on_background_service.dart at step 4a (BEFORE the existing Reinhard LAB transfer); Reinhard becomes a residual when the model output already matches the bg palette. Inputs: 'composite' [1,3,256,256] float32 ImageNet-normalized + 'mask' [1,1,256,256] float32 {0,1}. Outputs: [1,8] float32 filter args. The harmonization plan (docs/harmonization_plan.md) originally proposed bundling this; OrtRuntime currently lacks bundled-ONNX support (it accepts only Flutter assets via TFLite/LiteRT today), so we ship as downloaded for parity with the other ONNX models. Verify and pin sha256 before release. Until then this entry is in the deferredDownloadables allow-list.
models.purpose: Harmonizer white-box filter regressor (Ke 2022). Drives the AI harmonisation tier in the compose-on-bg flow.

models.$comment: Phase XVI.55: NAFNet-32 FP16 ONNX (Chen et al. 2022, ECCV — github.com/megvii-research/NAFNet). 'Nonlinear Activation Free Network' deblur model. ~9 MB FP16 — picked over MIMO-UNet+ on quality (33.71 dB GoPro PSNR vs 32.45) and ecosystem maturity. Single ONNX replaces Topaz Sharpen AI's three-model split (motion / focus / softness). Inputs: 'input' [1,3,512,512] float32 in [0,1] CHW. Outputs: [1,3,512,512] float32 in [0,1] CHW — clean image direct (no residual subtraction needed; NAFNet predicts the deblurred output, not noise). Source URL: huggingface.co/onnx-community/NAFNet-32-deblur-FP16/resolve/main/onnx/model.onnx (verify export exists when actually downloading). The URL field stays null and sha256 placeholder until verified via `shasum -a 256` and pinned. Until then this entry is in the deferredDownloadables allow-list.
models.purpose: NAFNet-32 deblur (Chen 2022). Drives the AI sharpen / deblur tier in the Detail panel.

models.$comment: Phase XVI.56: RestoreFormer++ FP16 ONNX (Wang et al. 2023 — github.com/wzhouxiff/RestoreFormerPlusPlus). Lighter cousin of GFPGAN/CodeFormer at ~75 MB FP16 vs GFPGAN-v1.4's ~340 MB FP32 — quality close to GFPGAN at a quarter of the size, especially strong on mild-to-moderate degradation. Inputs: 'input' [1,3,512,512] float32 in [-1,1] (mean=[0.5,0.5,0.5], std=[0.5,0.5,0.5]). Outputs: [1,3,512,512] float32 in [-1,1] — restored face. The service detects faces, square-crops each one with 30% padding, runs inference, and pastes the restored faces back into the source. Verify URL + sha256 of the community ONNX export before release. Until then this entry is in the deferredDownloadables allow-list.
models.purpose: RestoreFormer++ face restoration (Wang 2023). Drives the 'Restore Faces' AI tier.

models.$comment: Phase XVI.57: PhotoWCT2 FP16 ONNX (Chiu & Gurari, WACV 2022 — github.com/chiutaiyin/PhotoWCT2). 7.05 M params, ~26 MB FP16. Photoreal style transfer — preserves CONTENT structure while transferring the AESTHETIC (palette, contrast, atmosphere) of a reference image. Does NOT add brushwork (that is the existing Magenta scaffold's job). Drives the 'Match scene' AI tier described in docs/harmonization_plan.md XVI.31. Inputs: 'content' [1,3,512,512] float32 in [0,1] CHW + 'style' [1,3,512,512] float32 in [0,1] CHW (no ImageNet normalization — the network's VGG-16 backbone handles its own preprocessing internally). Outputs: [1,3,512,512] float32 in [0,1] CHW — photoreal stylised content. Verify URL + sha256 of the community ONNX export before release. Until then this entry is in the deferredDownloadables allow-list.
models.purpose: PhotoWCT2 photoreal style transfer (Chiu & Gurari 2022). 'Match scene aesthetic' tier alongside the existing Magenta scaffold.

models.$comment: Phase XVI.58: MobileViT-v2-0.5 INT8 ONNX (Mehta & Rastegari, ICLR 2022 — github.com/apple/ml-cvnets). ~7 MB INT8 — smallest of the MobileViT-v2 family, plenty for similarity retrieval at the 'For You' rail. Inputs: 'pixel_values' [1,3,256,256] float32 ImageNet-normalised (mean=[0.485,0.456,0.406], std=[0.229,0.224,0.225]). Outputs: [1,D] pooled feature vector (D is encoder-specific, typically 256 or 384 — opaque to the suggester; we just L2-normalise and dot-product against the pre-baked preset library). Source URL: huggingface.co/onnx-community/mobilevitv2-0.5/resolve/main/onnx/model_int8.onnx (verify export exists when actually downloading). The URL field stays null and sha256 placeholder until verified via `shasum -a 256` and pinned. Until then this entry is in the deferredDownloadables allow-list.
models.purpose: MobileViT-v2-0.5 image embedder (Mehta & Rastegari 2022). Drives the 'For You' preset rail via cosine-similarity kNN against pre-baked preset embeddings.
